sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The path is getting shorter:
- White at (21, 39) - ring center
- Magenta at (29, 36)
- Dark gray at (37, 33)
This is even shorter! The dark gray is now at (37, 33), which is much closer to the ring at (21, 39). Let me continue: click magenta, then click dark gray, then click ring again.
The dark gray seems to be moving up toward the ring each time. Maybe the goal is to get the dark gray all the way to the ring position?

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Shorter path: white at (21,39), magenta at (29,36), dark gray at (37,33)
# Advance and swap
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 29, 'col': 36}])
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 37, 'col': 33}])

seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
print("After advance+swap:")
for n in seg:
    if n['pixels'] < 50 and n['color'] not in ['g', 'B']:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        center = (sum(rows)//len(rows), sum(cols)//len(cols))
        print(f"  color={n['color']}, px={n['pixels']}, center={center}, children={n['children']}")
print(f"Done: {last_action_result.get('done')}, Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")

# Click ring again
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 21, 'col': 39}])

seg2 = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
print("\nAfter ring click:")
for n in seg2:
    if n['pixels'] < 50 and n['color'] not in ['g', 'B']:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        center = (sum(rows)//len(rows), sum(cols)//len(cols))
        print(f"  color={n['color']}, px={n['pixels']}, center={center}, children={n['children']}")
print(f"Done: {last_action_result.get('done')}, Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
